task_description: Compress all files in a folder into a ZIP archive and email that archive as an attachment to a specified recipient.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blank dark area; the nearby visible text is part of the file list, including entries like text2.txt, text3.txt, and text4.txt, but the X is not on any filename, icon, button, or editor text. In the full-screen view, Visual Studio Code is open in a dark theme with package.json active in the editor, the Explorer panel on the left listing a folder named test and files package.json, text1.txt, text2.txt, text3.txt, and text4.txt, and a chat/sidebar panel on the right. There is no context menu visible yet, so the right-click is occurring on empty background space in the file explorer area rather than on a specific object.
caption.action: Right-click the blank dark area in the Explorer file list, between the listed text files rather than on any filename or icon.
caption.expectation: A context menu for the Explorer background should appear, likely containing view and file-management options for the folder area rather than actions for a specific file.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the context menu item labeled “在文件资源管理器中显示,” with the shortcut “Shift+Alt+R” visible to its right. In the full-screen view, Visual Studio Code is open with a dark theme, package.json is active in the editor, the Explorer panel on the left lists the test folder and several text files, and the background context menu from the previous right-click is open over the Explorer area.
caption.action: Click the context menu item labeled “在文件资源管理器中显示”.
caption.expectation: The context menu should close and the current folder should open in the system file manager.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the full File Explorer window with a details view of the folder contents; the row for “package.json” is highlighted in light blue, indicating it is currently selected. Other visible items include the folders “.git” and “test” and the files “text1.txt,” “text2.txt,” “text3.txt,” and “text4.txt.” There is no text cursor or active text-entry field visible, so this hotkey applies to the current file list selection rather than to typed text. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is the active foreground window, opened to the folder “file1,” while Visual Studio Code remains visible behind it. The Ctrl+A hotkey would act on the Explorer file pane and replace the single-file selection with selection of all listed items in the folder.
caption.action: Press Ctrl+A to select every item in the current File Explorer file list.
caption.expectation: All visible files and folders in the current folder should become highlighted as selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the row for “text1.txt,” specifically over the selected file row rather than on blank whitespace. Nearby selected rows include “package.json,” “text2.txt,” and other items, indicating the click is on one of the currently highlighted selected files. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder “file1,” and all seven items in the file list are highlighted in light blue, showing they are selected. No context menu is currently visible, so this right-click is being performed on a selected file within a multi-selection; the resulting menu should be an object action menu for the selected files, typically including actions such as open, cut, copy, delete, rename, properties, compression or sharing options, and “show more options,” rather than a blank-area view/sort menu.
caption.action: Right-click the selected file row labeled “text1.txt” in the file list.
caption.expectation: A context menu for the selected items should appear, offering actions that apply to the selected files and folders.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a cascading context submenu, and the semitransparent red X is centered on the menu item labeled “添加到 ‘file1.zip’”. This is a UI control inside a submenu, not a file row or blank area. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder “file1” with all seven items still highlighted, and a right-click context menu is open with the “360压缩” submenu expanded to the right, showing several compression options.
caption.action: Click the submenu item labeled “添加到 ‘file1.zip’”.
caption.expectation: The menus should close and the selected items should be compressed into a new archive named “file1.zip” in the current folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Windows taskbar icons, with a semitransparent red X centered on the Google Chrome icon, a circular red-yellow-green-blue browser logo. This is a taskbar application button, which is a UI control in the bottom bar. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder “file1,” now showing a newly created archive named “file1.zip” among the files, while Visual Studio Code is visible behind Explorer. Clicking the Chrome taskbar icon should switch focus away from Explorer and open or bring forward Google Chrome.
caption.action: Click the Google Chrome taskbar icon.
caption.expectation: Google Chrome should become the active window, either launching if it is not open or coming to the foreground if it is already running.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Chrome bookmarks bar, with a semitransparent red X centered on the bookmark labeled “QQ邮箱,” next to a round multicolor icon. This is a clickable UI control in the browser's top area, not blank space. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on a new tab page in Chinese with the Google logo, the central search box, and several shortcut tiles; the bookmarks bar is visible beneath the address bar. Clicking this bookmark should select it and navigate the current tab to the QQ Mail website.
caption.action: Click the bookmark labeled “QQ邮箱” on the bookmarks bar.
caption.expectation: The current tab should start loading the QQ Mail page, replacing the new tab page content.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the left sidebar of QQ Mail, with a semitransparent red X centered on the large blue button labeled “写信,” which also has a small white paper-and-pencil compose icon. This is a UI control in the navigation area. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to the QQ Mail inbox page, showing the mailbox folders on the left, a search bar at the top, and a list of emails in the main pane.
caption.action: Click the blue “写信” compose button in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The inbox view should switch to the mail composition interface, opening a new message draft with fields for recipients, subject, and message body.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered in the large blank white area directly below the formatting toolbar, above the lower portion of the compose pane. It is not on a button, icon, or menu; it is on the email body editing region where message content can be entered. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to QQ Mail's compose page with fields for recipient and subject at the top, formatting controls including 图片, 插入, 导入文档, and the large message body area occupying most of the center. The click target is a blank editable canvas region in the compose editor.
caption.action: Click inside the blank message body area of the email compose editor.
caption.expectation: The editor should gain focus and place a text cursor in the message body, ready for typing the email content.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible text entry area is the recipient field labeled “收件人,” near the top of the QQ Mail compose form. The email address “1348952181@qq.co” is currently shown there, indicating the typed recipient is being entered into the To field and appears to be appending or still in progress rather than replacing selected text. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to QQ Mail's compose page with the left sidebar, send/preview/attachment controls above, a blank subject line labeled “主 题,” a large empty message body below, and a contacts panel on the right.
caption.expectation: The recipient field should contain the entered email address, allowing the draft to proceed with subject and message content.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray toolbar button labeled “附件,” with a black paperclip icon and a small downward arrow on its right edge. This is a toolbar control in the top action row of the QQ Mail compose page. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to QQ Mail’s message composition screen, with the recipient field already filled, a blank subject line, the message editor below, and the contacts list on the right.
caption.action: Click the “附件” attachment button with the paperclip icon in the compose toolbar.
caption.expectation: An attachment selection interface should open, such as a file picker or attachment menu, so a file can be chosen and added to the email.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the folder row labeled “file1” with a yellow folder icon in the left navigation pane of a Windows Open dialog. This is a selectable folder item, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, the Open file picker is in front of Chrome on the QQ Mail compose page. The dialog is currently browsing a folder path ending in “src,” the main file list in the center shows files such as bricks.png, nyra.obj, nyra.png, obj.js, project2.html, project2.js, and teapot.obj, and the left pane includes folders such as 程序分析 and 屏幕截图. The click target is the already highlighted “file1” folder in the sidebar.
caption.action: Click the “file1” folder in the left navigation pane of the Open dialog.
caption.expectation: The folder item should become selected or remain selected, updating focus to that location in preparation for choosing a file from it.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the row labeled “file1.zip,” which has a compressed ZIP archive icon and is highlighted in the file list of a Windows Open dialog. This is a selectable file item, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, the Open dialog is in front of Chrome on a QQ Mail compose window, browsing inside the “file1” folder; the file list includes .git, test, file1.zip, package.json, and several text files, with the “打开(O)” button available at the bottom.
caption.action: Click the “file1.zip” file in the Open dialog’s file list.
caption.expectation: The ZIP file should become selected or remain selected as the chosen attachment file, ready to be opened and attached.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue-outlined button labeled “打开(O)” in the lower-right area of a Windows Open dialog. This is a UI control button, not a blank region. In the full-screen view, the Open dialog is in front of Chrome on a QQ Mail compose page, the folder “file1” is open, and the file “file1.zip” is selected in the file list with its name also shown in the filename field at the bottom.
caption.action: Click the “打开(O)” button in the Open dialog.
caption.expectation: The dialog should close and the selected ZIP file should begin attaching to the email compose window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue button labeled “发送,” with a paper-plane icon on it near the top of the email compose panel. This is a toolbar/control button. In the full-screen view, QQ Mail is open in Chrome on a compose page with recipient “1348952181,” subject “file1,” and an attached file “file1.zip” already listed below the message body area.
caption.expectation: The email should be sent immediately, typically causing the compose view to close or switch to a sent-success confirmation/state.
